description: Push-to-talk walkie-talkie for the fleet. The Maude voice client presented as a Bard plugin: crews join a channel and talk, with a squelch gate to keep noisy jobsites from holding the channel open.

channel.description: Channel the device joins on enable, e.g. "crew-north-site".

pushToTalk.description: If true, transmit only while the talk key is held — recommended for very loud sites.

squelch.description: VOX squelch gate (features.md #66): suppress transmission/playback below a configurable noise floor so jobsite ambient noise (compressors, saws, traffic) doesn't keep the channel open.

enabled.description: Master switch for the squelch gate.

threshold.description: Noise floor in dBFS. Audio below this level is gated. Higher (toward 0) = more aggressive on a loud site.

side.description: Gate transmission (sender — saves relay bandwidth), playback (receiver), or both.

autoCalibrate.description: Continuously adapt threshold to the measured ambient level instead of a fixed dial.
